entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A staple crop providing dietary energy and mineral micronutrients in arid regions of Asia and Africa.

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: Arid regions where pearl millet is grown as a staple crop.

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Arid regions where pearl millet is grown as a staple crop.

entities.name: HEAT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of pearl millet to withstand and maintain productivity under high temperature conditions.

entities.name: INFRARED THERMAL IMAGING
entities.description: A rapid, non-destructive screening tool used to measure canopy temperature as a proxy for heat tolerance.

entities.name: IRON CONTENT
entities.description: The level of iron in pearl millet genotypes, categorized as high-Fe or low-Fe.

entities.name: IRON DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of iron within the grains of pearl millet.

entities.name: ZINC DENSITY
entities.description: The concentration of zinc within the grains of pearl millet.

entities.name: PANICLE TEMPERATURE DEPRESSION
entities.description: The difference between panicle temperature and ambient temperature, used to assess heat tolerance.

entities.name: FLAG LEAF TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature of the flag leaf, used to compare vegetative and reproductive heat stress responses.

entities.name: HIGH VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: A condition of high atmospheric moisture demand that affects canopy temperature.

entities.name: LOW VAPOR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: A condition of low atmospheric moisture demand.

entities.name: STIGMA STAGE
entities.description: A reproductive growth stage of pearl millet where panicle temperature is critical.

entities.name: SEED-SET STAGE
entities.description: A reproductive growth stage following the stigma stage.

entities.name: GRAIN-FILLING STAGE
entities.description: A growth stage where grains accumulate nutrients and are subject to temperature effects.

entities.name: HEAT STRESS
entities.description: Environmental stress caused by high temperatures that affects reproductive and vegetative parts of the plant.

entities.name: STIGMA RECEPTIVITY
entities.description: The ability of the stigma to receive pollen, which is maintained by cooler panicle temperatures.

entities.name: POLLEN VIABILITY
entities.description: The ability of pollen to remain functional, which is maintained by cooler panicle temperatures.
